ELR Name: VICTORIA(EASTERN LINES) - RAMSGATE (VIA HERNE HILL & CHATHAM)
Free Text: Limited records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency the station has contact details. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: VICTORIA(EASTERN LINES) - RAMSGATE (VIA HERNE HILL & CHATHAM)
Free Text: All rail tracks at Victoria Station are electrified by 750-volt DC electric traction supply cables from track level third rails. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified lines.

Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cable

Free Text: The site is composed of dense and scattered scrub <hawthorn, gorse and wayfaring tree>, woodland <oak, ivy, juniper, birch and dogwood> and areas of open chalk grassland <dwarf mouse ear, cat-mint, false brome and cock's foot> with a golf course and public open space. The various habitats support populations of butterflies <chalkhill blue>, moths, bugs, and beetles. 57 bird species have been recorded in the area, including stonechat, grass-hopper warbler, tawny owl, lesser whitethroat and greater and lesser spotted woodpecker.
